profile.name: Sai Roshan Rao Nelavalli
profile.summary: MS Computer Science graduate from Arizona State University (GPA 4.0, December 2025) with production internship experience and a deep project portfolio spanning distributed systems, AI/ML, backend engineering, VLSI/EDA tooling, and data engineering. Built a Raft-consensus KV store in Go, a real-time collaborative code editor with CRDTs, RAG systems over 30,000+ documents, Linux Kernel Modules, and VLSI physical design tools. Currently on F1/OPT, available to start immediately.

experiences.company: Arizona State University - Canvas LMS & AI Platform
bullets: Led end-to-end development of an LLM-powered AI chatbot and full-stack Learning Management System (React.js, Django, PostgreSQL) in a microservices architecture, improving student support scalability for 10,000+ users.

Maintained and optimized Kubernetes-based production backend services in a globally distributed Canvas LMS ecosystem, improving availability and reducing response latency across a multi-region university infrastructure.
Automated CI/CD pipelines with Docker and GitHub Actions on Linux/GCP, cutting release cycles by 25% and enabling zero-downtime continuous deployments.
Engineered fault-tolerant RESTful APIs with idempotent request patterns and TCP/IP health checks, ensuring high-concurrency system resilience across distributed workloads.
Designed and deployed microservices (Django, PostgreSQL) with clean API contracts and service-level isolation supporting 10,000+ concurrent users.

experiences.company: Tessellate Systems - Distributed Cloud Analytics Platform
bullets: Increased data-processing throughput by 25% by profiling and optimizing core C++/Python services on a distributed analytics platform hosted on AWS and Azure.
Reduced deployment time by 30% by containerizing services with Docker and standardizing multi-service CI/CD rollout pipelines.
Built Flask REST APIs with retry/backoff logic, health checks, and TCP/IP monitoring, strengthening service uptime and reliability.
Applied low-level performance engineering to distributed cloud data services, driving measurable throughput improvements across AWS and Azure environments.

experiences.company: Arizona State University - School of Computing & Augmented Intelligence
bullets: Assisted instruction of CSE 205 (Data Structures & Algorithms) for 200+ undergraduate students covering arrays, linked lists, stacks, queues, trees, heaps, hash tables, and graphs.

Graded programming assignments and exams assessing algorithm correctness, time/space complexity analysis (Big-O), and implementations of sorting algorithms (QuickSort, MergeSort, HeapSort) and graph traversals (BFS, DFS).

Held weekly office hours helping students debug Java implementations, reason through recursion and dynamic programming problems, and apply data structure selection strategies to real problem scenarios.
Conducted 500+ individual debugging and concept sessions, supporting students through shortest path algorithms (Dijkstra, Bellman-Ford), greedy algorithms, and divide-and-conquer patterns.

experiences.job_title: Teaching Assistant - Software Quality Assurance & Testing (CSE 464)
experiences.company: Arizona State University - School of Computing & Augmented Intelligence
bullets: Assisted instruction of CSE 464 (Software Quality Assurance & Testing) for 200+ students covering black-box and white-box testing, functional and structural testing, integration/regression testing, and software verification & validation.

Graded assignments applying industry-standard QA methodologies including equivalence partitioning, boundary value analysis, critical path analysis, decision table testing, and definition-use (D-U) path testing.
Guided hands-on labs using JUnit (unit testing), Selenium (automated UI and regression testing), and SonarQube (static code analysis, code quality metrics, technical debt tracking).
Supported students in applying software configuration management, bug lifecycle management, and test planning and documentation practices aligned with real-world QA workflows.
Held office hours reinforcing control flow testing, mutation testing, model-based testing, and software quality metrics.

experiences.job_title: Capstone Project Lead - Smart Sound-to-Speech Interpreter
experiences.company: Arizona State University - Fulton Schools of Engineering Capstone
bullets: Led end-to-end design and development of a Smart Sound-to-Speech Interpreter combining hardware sensors, embedded signal processing, and ML-based audio classification under faculty sponsorship through ASU's Fulton Schools Capstone Program.
Integrated hardware components with an ML audio processing pipeline to translate environmental and speech sounds into interpretable output in real time.
Collaborated with a cross-functional team across a full academic year, delivering a working prototype at the ASU Fulton Schools Capstone Showcase and iterating based on faculty mentor feedback.
Applied full SDLC: requirements engineering, system design, prototype fabrication, iterative testing, and validation across two semesters.

projects.title: dkvgo - Distributed Key-Value Store
projects.description: Fault-tolerant distributed KV store in Go with Raft consensus, consistent hashing, gRPC transport, and REST API.
outcomes: Built a fault-tolerant distributed KV store in Go implementing the Raft consensus protocol for leader election and log replication, with consistent hashing and virtual nodes for horizontal data partitioning and load balancing.

Designed a custom gRPC transport layer for inter-node RPC communication with heartbeat-based failure detection; exposed a REST API supporting GET/PUT/DELETE with configurable consistency levels (eventual vs. strong).
Built a benchmark suite measuring read/write latency, throughput, and failure recovery time across multi-node cluster configurations.

projects.title: Real-Time Collaborative Code Editor
projects.description: Google Docs-style collaborative code editor with CRDTs, WebSockets, sub-100ms sync latency, Monaco editor, OAuth, Docker Compose deployment.
outcomes: Built a Google Docs-style collaborative code editor supporting 50+ concurrent editing sessions at sub-100ms sync latency using CRDTs (Conflict-free Replicated Data Types) for conflict-free state synchronization.
Implemented Monaco editor with multi-cursor support, syntax highlighting, OAuth authentication, document version history with diff viewing, and room-based permission controls.
Deployed with Docker Compose (frontend, backend, Redis pub/sub for real-time messaging, PostgreSQL for persistence).

projects.title: End-to-End Data Pipeline with Quality Gates
projects.description: Production-style ELT pipeline with dbt transformation layers, Great Expectations quality gates, Airflow orchestration, and Metabase/Superset dashboards.
outcomes: Built a production-style ELT pipeline ingesting from 3+ heterogeneous sources (REST API, CSV/Parquet, CDC stream via Debezium); implemented dbt transformation layer with modular staging → intermediate → mart SQL models.
Added automated data quality gates using Great Expectations: schema validation, freshness checks, statistical anomaly detection, and referential integrity at every pipeline stage.

Orchestrated with Apache Airflow DAGs including retry logic, SLA monitoring, and failure alerting; built data lineage tracking and served final data through a REST API and Metabase/Superset dashboard.

projects.title: buddy-agent - Autonomous AI Agent
projects.description: Autonomous AI agent with multi-step reasoning, tool calling, and memory for executing complex tasks through LLM orchestration.
outcomes: Developed an autonomous AI agent with multi-step reasoning, tool calling, and persistent memory, capable of executing complex multi-stage tasks through LLM orchestration and structured agentic workflows.
Implemented tool-use patterns, prompt engineering strategies, and agent evaluation metrics to benchmark task completion accuracy, reliability, and hallucination rate across task categories.

projects.title: Static Timing Analysis (STA) Engine
projects.description: Timing analysis tool parsing gate-level Verilog netlists into a DAG and computing arrival times, required times, setup/hold slack, and critical paths.
outcomes: Building a timing analysis tool that parses gate-level Verilog netlists and Liberty (.lib) timing models into a DAG; implements topological-sort-based forward/backward traversal for arrival/required time propagation and calculates setup/hold slack to identify critical paths.

Adding multi-clock domain handling, incremental timing update capability (recompute only affected paths after a change), and industry-standard timing report generation matching Synopsys/Cadence STA tool output formats.

projects.title: Power Grid IR-Drop Analyzer
projects.description: Chip power delivery network simulator solving sparse resistive mesh system Gx=i via Conjugate Gradient/GMRES to generate IR-drop heatmaps.
outcomes: Implementing a chip power delivery network simulator modeling the power grid as a resistive mesh; solves the sparse linear system Gx=i using Conjugate Gradient/GMRES iterative solvers for voltage drop analysis.
Generating IR-drop heatmaps identifying voltage violation hotspots; optimizing for 1M+ node grids using Compressed Sparse Row (CSR) format for memory efficiency.

projects.title: User-Space TCP Stack
projects.description: TCP implemented from scratch on Linux raw sockets: 3-way handshake, reliable data transfer, sliding window flow control, Reno-style congestion control.
outcomes: Implementing TCP from scratch on Linux raw sockets: 3-way handshake (SYN/SYN-ACK/ACK), reliable data transfer with sequence numbers, acknowledgments, and retransmission timers.

Adding sliding window flow control and Reno-style congestion control (slow start, congestion avoidance, fast retransmit); benchmarking against the kernel TCP stack to visualize congestion window dynamics.

projects.title: Container Runtime from Scratch
projects.description: Minimal Docker-like container runtime using Linux namespaces, cgroups v2, overlay filesystem, and OCI-compatible image pulling.
outcomes: Building a minimal Docker-like container runtime using Linux namespaces (PID, NET, MNT, UTS, IPC) for process isolation and cgroup v2 for CPU/memory/I/O resource limits.
Implementing filesystem isolation with pivot_root/chroot and overlay filesystem; adding OCI-compatible image pulling, veth-pair networking, and a CLI: mycontainer run <image> <command>.

projects.title: URL Shortener at Scale - System Design in Practice
projects.description: Production URL shortener handling 10K+ req/sec with consistent hashing, Bloom filter collision detection, Redis caching, analytics pipeline, and Kubernetes HPA.
outcomes: Building a production URL shortener handling 10,000+ requests/sec: consistent hashing for horizontal scaling, base62 encoding with Bloom filter collision detection, Redis cache-aside with TTL-based eviction for hot URLs.

Adding click analytics pipeline (user agent, geo-IP, referrer streamed to analytics store), token-bucket rate limiting per API key, and k6 load testing demonstrating p99 latency <50ms on Kubernetes with HPA.

projects.title: LLM Evaluation & Red-Teaming Framework
projects.description: Configurable LLM evaluation pipeline with automated metrics for hallucination, toxicity, instruction-following, and adversarial robustness across multiple model APIs.
outcomes: Building a configurable LLM evaluation pipeline running prompt suites against multiple APIs (OpenAI, Anthropic, open-source); implementing automated metrics for factual consistency (NLI-based), toxicity scoring, hallucination detection, and instruction-following rate.
Adding adversarial prompt generation for systematic jailbreak/injection testing, comparison dashboards with statistical significance tests, and regression detection across model versions.

projects.title: ML Model Serving Infrastructure
projects.description: Low-latency model serving system with dynamic batching, blue-green deployments, semantic caching, A/B testing, and p50/p95/p99 Prometheus monitoring.
outcomes: Building a low-latency model serving system with dynamic request batching for GPU utilization, blue-green deployment with automatic rollback on quality regression, and semantic caching for near-duplicate query deduplication.
Implementing A/B testing framework routing traffic between model versions; monitoring dashboard tracking p50/p95/p99 latency, throughput, and model drift signals.

projects.title: Distributed Tracing System - Mini Jaeger
projects.description: OpenTelemetry-compatible distributed tracing system with gRPC span collection, time-series storage, Gantt-chart trace UI, and automated p99 anomaly detection.
outcomes: Building an OpenTelemetry-compatible distributed tracing system: trace context propagation (trace ID, span ID, parent span ID) across HTTP and gRPC; a gRPC-based trace collector with time-series-optimized storage backend.

Implementing a web UI with Gantt-chart trace visualization, service dependency graph, and latency histograms; automated anomaly detection flagging traces where latency exceeds p99 by 2x or error rate spikes.

projects.title: Chaos Engineering Framework
projects.description: Automated failure injection system with YAML experiment DSL, pluggable failure agents, orchestrator lifecycle, and blast radius safety controls.
outcomes: Building an automated failure injection system with pluggable agents for network latency/partition (tc/iptables), process kill, CPU/memory stress, and disk I/O saturation - controlled via a YAML experiment DSL.

Implementing orchestrator lifecycle: baseline metrics → inject failure → observe → verify hypothesis → rollback; safety controls with auto-abort on threshold breach and mandatory blast radius limiting.

projects.title: Linux Performance Profiler - eBPF Dashboarding Tool
projects.description: Kernel-level eBPF profiling tool measuring syscall latency, TCP retransmits, disk I/O, memory allocation hot paths, and scheduler events - visualized via Prometheus/Grafana.
outcomes: Building a kernel-level performance profiling tool using eBPF (via bcc/libbpf) to attach probes measuring syscall latency, scheduler events, TCP retransmits, disk I/O latency per process, and memory allocation hot paths.

Exporting aggregated eBPF histogram/counter data as Prometheus metrics; building Grafana dashboards with drill-down from system overview to per-process/per-function metrics and a CLI: myprof trace <pid> --duration 30s.

projects.title: Real-Time Streaming Analytics Platform
projects.description: Kafka + Flink streaming platform processing 1,000+ events/sec with windowed aggregations, exactly-once semantics, anomaly detection, and S3/MinIO data lake sink.
outcomes: Building a Kafka-based streaming platform processing 1,000+ events/sec with a Flink application for real-time windowed aggregations (tumbling, sliding, session windows) and exactly-once semantics via Kafka transactions.

Adding real-time anomaly detection (z-score / isolation forest) on streaming aggregates, WebSocket+React live dashboard, MinIO/S3 data lake sink, backpressure handling, and dead-letter queues for malformed events.

projects.title: Change Data Capture (CDC) Replication System
projects.description: CDC system using PostgreSQL WAL-based logical replication publishing to Kafka with exactly-once delivery, schema evolution, and multi-sink replication.
outcomes: Building a CDC system using PostgreSQL logical replication slots (WAL-based change capture); custom connector reads WAL events, transforms them, and publishes to Kafka topics with exactly-once delivery and idempotent consumers.

Adding schema evolution handling (new columns, type changes) without breaking downstream consumers; multi-sink replication to Elasticsearch, ClickHouse, and data lake; monitoring for replication lag and consistency verification.

projects.title: CI/CD Pipeline Engine - Mini GitHub Actions
projects.description: YAML-driven CI/CD engine executing pipeline steps in isolated Docker containers with DAG-based parallel scheduling, artifact passing, and GitHub webhook triggers.
outcomes: Building a YAML-driven CI/CD engine parsing workflow files with stages, steps, and dependencies; executing each step in an isolated Docker container with DAG-based parallel scheduling for independent steps.
Implementing artifact passing between steps, a web dashboard with live log streaming via WebSockets, and GitHub webhook triggers to automatically start pipelines on push events.

projects.title: VLSI Standard Cell Placer
projects.description: Multi-stage C++ placement engine using Leiden graph clustering and simulated annealing with HPWL optimization, benchmarked against ISPD datasets.
outcomes: Built a multi-stage C++ placement engine: parsed industry-standard netlists into hypergraph data structures, applied the Leiden community detection algorithm for graph-based clustering, then ran simulated annealing with HPWL cost function for fine-grained cell placement optimization.
Integrated a Tcl CLI mimicking OpenROAD-style EDA tool workflows; benchmarked against ISPD competition datasets and visualized placement results with Python/matplotlib wire-density heatmaps.

projects.title: SupplementalRxAI - RAG & Knowledge Graph System
projects.description: Production RAG system processing 30,000+ medical documents with FAISS vector search and Neo4j knowledge graphs.
outcomes: Engineered a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) architecture to process 30,000+ medical documents, integrating FAISS vector search and Neo4j knowledge graphs to minimize LLM hallucinations.
Built an automated data pipeline using Selenium for web scraping and a Flask/React interface to enable real-time evidence retrieval and citation tracking.

projects.title: Linux Kernel Memory Manager (LKM)
projects.description: Linux Kernel Module in C with 5-level page table walking and hrtimer-based memory profiling reporting RSS, SWAP, and WSS.
outcomes: Developed a custom Linux Kernel Module in C to analyze process virtual memory by parsing task_struct data, providing page-level usage insights.
Implemented a 5-level page table walker and hrtimer-based sampling to report RSS, SWAP, and WSS metrics with minimal profiling overhead.

projects.title: Distributed Geo-Spatial Data Pipeline (NYC Taxi Data)
projects.description: Kafka/ZooKeeper streaming pipeline ingesting Parquet datasets into Neo4j with PageRank and BFS optimization.
outcomes: Architected a scalable data ingestion workflow using Apache Kafka and ZooKeeper to stream Parquet datasets into a Neo4j graph database for geospatial analysis.
Implemented graph algorithms (PageRank, BFS) to optimize query performance on heavy data loads.

projects.title: C++ 2D Thermal Modeler (VLSI Power Analysis)
projects.description: Unix-based C++ FDM thermal simulation for chip power analysis and hotspot identification.
outcomes: Engineered a Unix-based C++ application to simulate multi-physics heat distribution across chip layouts for hotspot identification and power analysis.
Implemented an iterative Finite-Difference Method (FDM) solver to solve complex physical design challenges.

projects.title: Distributed Chat Application with Real-Time Analytics
projects.description: Scalable real-time messaging platform for 1,000+ users at sub-60ms latency with 98.9% delivery reliability and live dashboards.
outcomes: Designed a scalable messaging platform for 1,000+ users with sub-60 ms message delivery using Flask, React.js, Redis, and AWS.
Achieved 98.9% message reliability and integrated live dashboards for real-time monitoring.

projects.title: Parallelized Academic Papers Search Engine
projects.description: Multithreaded Java/Apache Lucene backend with sub-second responses across 100,000+ documents and 40% throughput improvement.
outcomes: Developed a multithreaded Java/Apache Lucene backend delivering sub-second query responses across 100,000+ documents.
Increased indexing throughput by 40% through data pipeline optimization and deployment on Linux servers.

education.degree: Master of Science in Computer Science
Bachelor of Science in Computer Science
